reasoning: Candidate (right panel): golden-bordered dialog box with parchment interior — warm, Zelda-adjacent, broadly on-tone for a Nintendo family-friendly fairy-tale target. No grimdark, no fan-service, no cold-corporate treatment in the candidate itself. However, the peer reference assets (left tileset) are a significant red flag: heavy grey stone dungeon tiles, red blood stains, dark teal portals, skull/bone debris, and fire-lava elements — a textbook desaturated dungeon-crawler palette that directly violates the bright/warm/whimsical target. This peer context signals systemic tonal drift in the broader asset library even if the dialog panel survives on its own merits. The candidate panel is also somewhat generic (plain dual-rectangle layout, standard RPG gold frame) — it passes the 'not grimdark' bar but lacks the toy-diorama warmth and distinctive fairy-tale charm (no organic curves, no playful motifs, no hand-crafted softness) needed to firmly anchor the Echoes/Hyrule target aesthetic. Score held at marginal pending asset library audit.
